Dear Nr. Paul:

LOS ANGELES, 90012 
This letter is to confirm advice given to you in my office 
on March 30, 1981. 
The facts as you relayed th~m to me are as follows: 
1. 
Each lodge of the Boilermakers Union does not make 
contributions or any political independent expenditure. 
2. 
All contributions and expenditures will be made by the 
International Union of Boilermakers. 
The International is 
located out-of-state. 
3. 
Funds used for these political contributions and 
expenditures will be raised by the local lodges by various 
means, i.e. sale of hats, buttons, raffles, etc. 
4. 
The lodges conducting the sales will deduct their 
expenses and forward the balance of the funds raised to the 
International for reporting purposes. 
The question you posed is what filing obligations are 
incurred by the lodges and the International Union? 
I advised that since the local lodges are merely acting 
<:IS "agents" for the International, they will not incur any 
filing obligation as long as the International completely reports 
all of the contributions received by the lodges and all 
contributions or expenditures made to California state and local 
officeholders, candidates, their committees or ballot measure 
committees. 
• 


:1r. 
:-~c Fco'Cl 
P.pril 9, 1981 
1'.:, e 
The International Union must file a Statement of Organization, 
Form 410 with the Secretary of State's office in California 
within ten days of receiving $500 in contributions allocated for 
California campaigns'. 
(Government Code Section 84101). 
Periodic 
campaigll statements are then required to be filed by the International's 
general purpose recipient committee pursuant to Government Code 
Section 84200. 
Form 420, Recipient Con~ittee Long form is to be 
filed with the Secretary of State (original plus one copy), Los 
Angeles County Registrar-Recorder (two copies) and San Francisco 
County (two copies). 
Government Code Section 84211 requi~es that 
each donor be identified by name, street address, occupation, 
employer date and amount recei~ed, and the cumulative amount of 
contributions. 
Therefore, the local Idoges raising the funds are 
requi~cd to maintain adequate records of their fundraising events 
in vrder tu provide the International with the necessary required 
information for the required campaign statements. 
(Government 
Code Section 84104) .. Detailed information on .... lhen and where to 
file the campaign statements is contained in the 1981 Informational 
Manual on pages 4 and 10. 
A copt of the Manual has been 
enclcsed for your use. 
1 hope this information has been helpful to you. 
Please do 
not hesitate to contact our office if I can be of any further 
assistance to you. 
lIPA:jg 
Enclosures:. (5)

Consultant

sections.question: What filing obligations are incurred by the lodges and the International Union?
sections.conclusion: Since the local lodges are merely acting as "agents" for the International, they will not incur any filing obligation as long as the International completely reports all of the contributions received by the lodges and all contributions or expenditures made to California state and local officeholders, candidates, their committees or ballot measure committees. The International Union must file a Statement of Organization, Form 410 with the Secretary of State's office in California within ten days of receiving $500 in contributions allocated for California campaigns. Periodic campaign statements are then required to be filed by the International's general purpose recipient committee pursuant to Government Code Section 84200 (Form 420, Recipient Committee Long form). The local lodges raising the funds are required to maintain adequate records of their fundraising events in order to provide the International with the necessary required information for the required campaign statements.
sections.question_synthetic: The Boilermakers Union lodges seek clarification on their filing obligations under California campaign finance law when they raise funds through various means and forward them to the International Union for political contributions and expenditures.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Local lodges acting as agents for the International Union have no direct filing obligations provided the International Union fully reports all contributions and expenditures. The International must file Form 410 upon receiving $500 in California contributions and subsequently file periodic Form 420 campaign statements, while local lodges must maintain detailed fundraising records for reporting purposes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question, facts, and conclusion sections. Document is dated April 9, 1981 and confirms in-office advice given March 30, 1981. Specific Government Code sections cited throughout. High confidence extraction due to clear structure and explicit question and conclusion sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What filing obligations are incurred by the lodges and the International Union?

CONCLUSION: Since the local lodges are merely acting as "agents" for the International, they will not incur any filing obligation as long as the International completely reports all of the contributions received by the lodges and all contributions or expenditures made to California state and local officeholders, candidates, their committees or ballot measure committees. The International Union must file a Statement of Organization, Form 410 with the Secretary of State's office in California within ten days of receiving $500 in contributions allocated for California campaigns. Periodic campaign statements are then required to be filed by the International's general purpose recipient committee pursuant to Government Code Section 84200 (Form 420, Recipient Committee Long form). The local lodges raising the funds are required to maintain adequate records of their fundraising events in order to provide the International with the necessary required information for the required campaign statements.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms that local Boilermakers Union lodges acting as agents for the International Union have no independent filing obligations, provided the International Union reports all contributions and expenditures. The International Union must file appropriate campaign finance disclosure forms and the local lodges must maintain adequate fundraising records.
